Off-Gas Treatment Overview
WWTP odour (H₂S, NH₃)15,000 Nm³/h
Process exhaust (NMHCs) × 2 lines2 × 6,000 Nm³/h
R&D lab exhaust (dust + organics)1,500 Nm³/h
All streams treated by water scrubbing → bio-trickling filter → alkaline/oxidative scrubbing. Discharge via 15–25 m stacks, complying with GB 31572-2015 Table 5.
2
Overall Treatment Process Flow
— Physical-chemical pre-treatment → Multi-stage biological → MBR → 2-stage RO + 3-effect evaporation
P&ID piping design
Off-gas treatment layout
Effluent monitoring room / pump room
Collection sump + pump sizing
3
My Design Contributions
— Four deliverables as junior engineer in the project team
Contribution 01
P&ID Piping Design
Produced piping and instrumentation diagrams for the full wastewater treatment train. Responsibilities included pipe sizing, material selection for acid/alkali resistance, valve and instrumentation placement, and control loop identification across all units (equalization tanks through RO system).
Key Design Info
ScopeAll treatment units
Pipe materialPP / 304SS / HDPE
Design stdHG/T 20519
InstrumentspH, DO, ORP, MLSS
DeliverableP&ID drawings
Contribution 02
Off-Gas Treatment System Layout
Designed equipment arrangement drawings for three independent exhaust treatment systems. Each train comprises spray towers, bio-trickling filter, secondary scrubber, draft fan, circulation pumps, and dosing system. Key challenge: fitting WWTP 15,000 Nm³/h + 2×6,000 Nm³/h process lines within constrained plot.
System Parameters
WWTP odour15,000 Nm³/h
Process off-gas2 × 6,000 Nm³/h
R&D exhaust1,500 Nm³/h
Tower (WWTP)Φ2200×6000H, PP
Bio-filter8500×6000×4000 FRP
Fan (WWTP)HF-361B 30 kW VFD
Stack ht.25 m (WWTP) / 15 m (process & R&D)
Contribution 03
Effluent Online Monitoring Room & Pump Room
Designed the effluent monitoring station and downstream pump room: instrument selection and layout (COD, pH, NH₃-N, TN, TP, conductivity, Parshall flume), civil integration, and equipment positioning. Effluent pump sized for discharge to municipal sewer connection.
Instruments & Equipment
COD analyser0–1,000 mg/L
pH analyser0–14
NH₃-N analyseronline, continuous
TN analyser0–200 mg/L
TP analyser0–20 mg/L
Flow meterParshall b=51 mm
Effluent pump65WQ20-25-4, 4 kW
Contribution 04
Collection Sump Design + Pump Hydraulic Sizing
Designed underground collection sump for WWTP drainage/leakage recovery. Performed hydraulic calculations to size the lift pump: flow rate and head determined from system curve analysis, pump model selected (65FSB-32 corrosion-resistant), verified against motor power with 1-duty-1-standby arrangement.
Design Specifications
Sump dims.3.0 × 6.0 × 2.5 m
StructureUnderground RC, waterproofed
Pump model65FSB-32 (acid-resistant)
Flow rate Q25 m³/h
Total head H32 m
Motor power5.5 kW
Arrangement1 duty + 1 standby
4

Effluent Quality vs Standard
ParameterInfluentEffluentRemoval
CODCr≤6,000≤60>98%
BOD₅~3,000≤20>99%
NH₃-N≤90≤8>91%
TN≤120≤40>67%
TP≤10≤1.0>90%

Effluent discharged to Toumengang Economic Development Zone municipal WWTP via indirect discharge. Online monitoring covers COD, pH, NH₃-N, TN, TP, conductivity, and flow.

38
Key Unit: MPDH Pool (Patented Process)
Multi-stage push-flow dual-layer anoxic reactor (Patent ZL2021207426763) combines micro-aerobic + anaerobic hydrolysis zones to break down PLA oligomers (low B/C ratio).
Effective vol.826 m³ × 2 trains
HRT59.4 h
MLSS5,000 mg/L
Vol. load0.98 kgCOD/m³·d
COD removal45%
MBR + 2-Stage RO
MBR vol.274 m³ × 2 · HRT 19.7 h
MLSS8,000 mg/L
MembraneSUKE-20 flat sheet × 14
RO recovery~75% · 2-stage series
3-effect evap.3.5 m³/h · 2205 SS
RO productCOD ≤38 mg/L
